preview.summary: Defensively solid Ecuador meet tournament debutants Curacao in a Group E clash of contrasting profiles. Ecuador's organized, low-conceding approach is favored against the smallest nation ever to reach a World Cup.
preview.homeForm: Ecuador qualified second in CONMEBOL above Brazil, Uruguay and Colombia, conceding just five goals in 18 games, and sit 23rd in the world.
preview.awayForm: Curacao topped their CONCACAF group unbeaten to become the smallest nation by population ever to qualify for a World Cup, on their fourth attempt.
preview.homeKey: Moisés Caicedo, world-class Chelsea midfield engine.
preview.awayKey: Gervane Kastaneer, who led Curacao's qualifying scoring with five goals.
storylines: Curacao make their first-ever World Cup appearance
Veteran Dick Advocaat guides the Curacao debutants
Ecuador's miserly defense conceded the fewest goals in South American qualifying
preview.h2h: The two nations have never met at senior level.
